rulings.Case Title: LAMAR D BURKS ET AL VS. TERRENCE LEROY SANDLES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Possession Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR POSSESSION OF REAL PROPERTY is continued to January 23, 2023 for the moving party to submit a supplemental brief addressing case authority regarding issuance of orders/writ of possession in partition actions on a motion. Brief shall not exceed 3 pages and shall be filed and served no later than January 16, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEANA PEREZ ET AL VS. BIANA CHERNOGUZ, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants? Special Motion To Strike New Complaint As Unlawful S.L.A.P.P. Suit
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS BIANA CHERNOGUZ AND VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO STRIKE NEW COMPLAINT AS UNLAWFUL S.L.A.P.P. SUIT is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden under the ANTI-SLAPP statute. Defendants appear to seek to strike the entire complaint (See Notice of Motion) and not any specific claims arising out of protected activities. Furthermore, moving party fails to show that the claims arise solely out of the service of the 60 Day Notice, i.e., that the service itself is the wrong complained about by the Plaintiffs. Additionally, assuming that some of the claims in the complaint arise out of protected activity, Plaintiffs provide sufficient evidence to demonstrate probability of prevailing on the merits. The Court also notes that the moving party appears to seek a determination of the propriety of allegations admittedly not arising out of protected activity (e.g. habitability) on this motion. No authority is cited for the proposition that the ANTI-SLAPP is a proper motion to challenge such allegations. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. JASON M. KAHRER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2022 line 8. DEFENDANT JASON KAHRER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Plaintiff must allege compliance with SFRO 37.9(o) regarding a tenancy alleged not to be subject to SFRO. Any factual dispute regarding applicability of SFRO is not properly addressed on a demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
